生於亂世，有種責任
身為教育工作者，於這樣的亂世，我們更責無旁貸
傳道授業解惑，是我們為師者的專業
但比起教授孩子學業上的知識
教育孩子正確的道德觀及價值觀更為重要
學生壞，不聽話，再生氣也不能打他們
學生打架，手無縛雞之力也要分開他們
這就是我們教育工作者的專業

當你累了亂發脾氣，用粗言穢語挑釁別人
當你用催淚彈、橡膠子彈、布袋彈對付手無寸鐵的民眾
當你要求撤離，卻又困著正在撤離的群眾
當你默許黑社會暴力行為
視而不見，聽而不聞
警察們，你們專業嗎？

當你立心不良，堅持送中，不考慮其他方案
當你不分青紅皂白，定性六一二為暴動
當你堅持不成立獨立調查委員會
當你每每為了死物，斥責示威者是暴徒
甚至認為國徽和玻璃比市民生命更重要
特首，您又專業嗎？

願我們都能在同一片土地
謹守自己的崗位
做到專業

懇請政府正視並正面回應這兩個月以來，
響徹整個香港，甚至全球的五大訴求︰
1. 全面撤回逃犯條例 Complete withdrawal of the extradition bill
2. 撤回6.12暴動定性 Retract the 12 June riot claim
3. 釋放所有被捕抗爭者並撤銷控罪 Free and drop charges against arrested protestors
4. 成立獨立調查委員會，徹查警隊濫權濫暴，徹查警黑勾結7.21元朗恐襲
Independent investigation of police brutality
5. 以行政命令解散立法會，立即實行雙普選 Universal suffrage

選擇的權利 我們曾經擁有
時間的流逝 被侵蝕得徹底
民主和自由 何以值得追求
井蛙與夏蟲 豈能看得通透

以黃色為記 紀錄雨傘後的美
以白花為念 念掛那無名的你
穿上黑衣 成為百萬中的一點
齊聲呼喊 震撼全世界

早知高牆冷寞 仍願以雞蛋一搏
儘管人性險惡 總不敵齊上齊落
羅馬非一日建成 不積步難至千里
懷着信念 哪怕水滴不能石穿
一直堅持
終能撼動萬千
站在我們這邊

香港人加油！

PS 記得唔好影到日期



講得幾好聽吖

懇請政府正視並正面回應這兩個月以來，
響徹整個香港，甚至全球的五大訴求︰
1. 全面撤回逃犯條例 Complete withdrawal of the extradition bill
2. 撤回6.12暴動定性 Retract the 12 June riot claim
3. 釋放所有被捕抗爭者並撤銷控罪 Free and drop charges against arrested protestors
4. 成立獨立調查委員會，徹查警隊濫權濫暴，徹查警黑勾結7.21元朗恐襲
Independent investigation of police brutality
5. 以行政命令解散立法會，立即實行雙普選 Universal suffrage

replies.msg:
各位香港市民：

我們是一群以香港為家的教育工作者。

每天都以「傳道、授業、解惑」為己任，盡力教好每一位學生。除教授書本上的知識外，亦不忘教授學生做人處世的道理與態度，更希望學生常存一顆追求普世價值、美好將來的赤子之心。

但直至剛剛過去的一個半月，我們之所見、所聞、所感令我們感到困惑，且與一直以來所相信的、所教授的、所傳承的理念與價值相違背。

在6月9日，100萬市民和平上街反修例，換來的只有不屑一顧，草案如期二讀，最後演變成612金鐘衝突。6月16日200萬市民上街；7月1日佔領立法會；7月13日光復上水；7月14日沙田商場圍捕血戰；7月21日上環毫無預警下向市民開三十六槍及元朗黑社會恐襲，我們感到憤怒至極而且痛心疾首！

一次又一次的和平示威，演化到有部分示威人士做出更激進的行為，更甚者致使多位年輕人為此賠上寶貴的生命，凡此種種都只為對惡法與不義的政權作出控訴，但換來的卻只有一而再、再而三的漠視與錄音機式不斷重複的記者會內容和譴責。

到7月21日晚上，元朗恐襲時警察對市民的輕蔑與袖手旁觀之態度；對施暴者的縱容與包庇，實實在在告訴我們這個不義政權的高傲與對人民的冷漠。

維持社會安定，維護社會公義，從來都是當權者的責任，但如今社會公義蕩然無存，政府帶頭有錯不認、有責不問、顛倒是非黑白，實為一眾莘莘學子作了一個最壞的榜樣！此舉令人髮指，特首林鄭月娥、一眾政府高官、親共派議員及冷漠之社會賢達確實責無旁貸、難辭其咎！

就香港警隊近日涉嫌嚴重違規、濫權，以至使用過份武力，更甚的是警黑勾結，香港政府須立即成立獨立調查委員會，全面徹查各事件，藉以還眾多受害者與被捕人士一個公道，此舉確實刻不容緩！

對於近日在家中發生的種種不公不義、荒謬絕倫的事件，作為一群以香港為家的教育工作者確實深感不滿與遺憾！

我們希望藉此重申我們對五大訴求的堅定立場，謹在此重申：

1.全面撤回逃犯條例
2.撤銷612暴動定性
3.撤銷對示威者之檢控
4.成立獨立調查委員會徹底追究警察濫權及警黑勾結問題
5.實行2020雙普選

一群以香港為家的教育工作者上

replies.msg:
香港新界將軍澳勤學里一號
香港華人基督教聯會真道書院
鍾嘉樂校監鈞鑒：

回應梁振英先生致信一事


　　久有仰聞　貴校自二零零二年建校至今，一直秉承創辦時的使命，不斷在學術與非學術方面追求進步，教學團隊專業，精益求精，配合現代教育趨勢的發展，追求卓越，桃李滿門，一直在區內享負盛名。

　　現今香港社會因修訂《逃犯條例》引起的風波未息，社會氣氛動盪，暴力加劇，社會撕裂。當權者未有聆聽市民訴求，一再回避是次政治議題之核心，以致事件演變成警民對立，衝突愈演愈烈，警民關係更接近仇視的狀態。作為一位專業的教育工作者，在大是大非之下更需抱著極為冷靜﹑理性及持平的角度分析現況，以發揮教師之專業。　貴校助理校長戴健暉先生發表意見的言辭過於偏激，有所失當是不爭的事實，雖其宣洩之言辭未有直接傷害他人，卻深化了雙方仇恨。惟戴老師後來收回言論並作公開致歉，已為此事負上了其作為教育工作者的社會責任，故及後校方如何處理事件應屬學校內務。

　　在將近兩個月的抗爭運動中，社會一直充斥着各種令人痛心疾首的事件：年輕人為爭取公義而受傷甚至犧牲性命，切盼可喚醒政府的良知; 無辜市民於公眾地方被懷疑三合會人士毆打，但警方視若無睹；警方使用不必要且不合比例的武力血腥鎮壓遊行人士的合理政治訴求，卻對眾目睽睽下手持武器的大批白衫人士並未採取即時的行動等等。一系列的事件為全港市民帶來一次又一次的痛心和震怒，即便如戴先生一名熱愛教育工作的人士，相信亦會感到悲哀及憤慨，情緒一時難以梳理亦能體諒。現階段實在不宜把事件的處理染上政治色彩，節上生枝，增添社會的負面情緒，作為教育工作者，我們更應關注莘莘學子潛藏的道德及情緒危機。

　　梁振英先生以香港特別行政區前行政長官及全國政協副主席落款，公然以職級身份施壓，態度囂張跋扈，更欲干涉學校內務，輕蔑校方的專業判斷。素仰 貴學一向本著管理自主，備有自我完善的工作循環，有策略地規劃工作及孕育反思文化，是促進學校持續發展和改善的關鍵，校內亦早已具備對教育工作者的評量標準，教師的專業及教學質素一直以來備受監督。如欲於此時因梁振英先生的一封公開信而革除 貴校助理校長戴健暉的職務，未免破壞了行之有效的校本管理，更會產生寒蟬效應，增添前線教育工作者及學子的心理壓力，製造白色恐怖。對梁卑躬屈膝不但令濫權者變本加厲，甚至破壞教育的本質，等同直接默許權貴間的迂腐及不公義，階級間的醜惡及不平等，試問日後將從何教授我們的下一代「社會人人平等，不分貧賤，互相體諒」的德行觀念？

　　學校於教育工作者而言是展現專業的地方，於學生而言是安心的學習環境，教學相長。教育工作者一直以授業解惑為己任，教學之道在乎明德理智，教授書本知識僅是基本，教授莘莘學子明辨事非，以史鑑今，抱誠守真，才是教育真正的追求。是次修例風波提供一個難得的平台供學子學習，嘗試了解、分析、體會及檢究各個不同持份者，社會上不同人士的聲音，而戴老師已為己誤作承擔，謙卑接受意見，已是最好的身教。難道排除異己，閉目塞耳，借故打擊一位失言的教育工作者，以保護之名隔絕及盲目地營造一個安穩的假象，比教授學生審時度勢，洞徹事理更為重要，更符合教育的本質？切望校方能慎思此事會為學生帶來怎樣的一課！

　　英國的若望‧亨利‧紐曼樞機（John Henry Newman）在《大學的觀念》一書中提及：「只有教育，才能使一個人對自己的觀點和判斷有清楚和自覺的認識；只有教育，才能令他/她闡明觀點時有道理，表達時有說服力，鼓動時有力量。」學校作為一個容許犯錯及改過的地方，是次事件正好教授學子面對問題的抗逆能力，學習何謂失敗者的優雅身姿；學校以外，成人的言行身教亦為重要，成人應以仁義之實踐為己任，以道德之展現為目標，才能言行均一讓學子建立合乎情理的個人價值觀，辨別是非，具「道之所在，雖千萬人吾往矣」的堅持。盼 貴校能本著以聖經真理辦學，以主耶穌的愛及寬恕待人處事，讓學生﹑教育工作者及香港市民藉以是次事件感受教育的美好及其真正的本質。

　 敬祝
均安
xxx敬上

二零一九年七月三十一日

July 31, 2019

Rev. Chung Ka Lok
School Supervisor
The Hong Kong Chinese Christian Churches Union Logos Academy
No. 1, Kan Hok Lane, Tseung Kwan O, Hong Kong

Dear Mr. Chung,
RE: CY Leung’s Letter

Since its establishment in 2002, HKCCCU Logos Academy has always adhered to its founding principles, striving for academic excellence and the provision of all-round education. Thanks to the unwavering dedication of professional teaching staff, your school is the cradle of social elite and is well reputed in Tseung Kwan O.

In the wake of the current social turmoil arising from the authority’s stubborn refusal to take into account society’s concerns about the extradition bill, violence has intensified and never has our society seen more animosity between citizens and the police. There is no denial that the opinions aired by your Assistant Principal Mr. Tai, which serve more as an outlet for anger than as a platform for rational discussion, are too radical to be within the confines of social acceptance. As a professional educator, Mr. Tai is expected to keep a cool head and analyse this issue in an objective manner, as opposed to venting his frustration. While his words may not have directly caused any harm, they do deepen hatred. Realising this, Mr. Tai has promptly retracted his speech and issued an apology, from which point the whole incident should be nothing more than the internal affairs of your school.

As the protests have now entered into the second month, more tragedies are also unfolding. More and more young adults demanding equal rights and justice have been injured during the protests. Some have even sacrificed their lives, in an attempt to rouse the government’s conscience. While innocent civilians were beaten by alleged triad members wearing white T-shirts in public areas, the police turned a blind eye and did not take any immediate actions. Instead, they used excessive force to disperse weaponless protesters. All this has saddened and infuriated Hongkongers. It is completely understandable that Mr Tai, a passionate educator, felt a deep sadness and bitter anger and reacted in a rather emotional manner. For the moment we should not make matters worse and pile more negative sentiments onto society. We must take steps to defuse the moral and emotional crisis our students are suffering.

In Mr. Leung’s letter, which saw him openly addressing himself as Former Chief Executive of Hong Kong and vice chairperson of the Chinerse People’s Political Consultative Conference, he attempted to interfere with the school’s internal affairs by appealing to the prestigious status of his public office. One could not help but flinch at Mr. Leung’s arrogance. Schools should enjoy managerial autonomy so that it can implement its own framework of strategic planning and rigorous evaluation in order to facilitate sustainable development. And there are guidelines for teachers’ professionalism and teaching quality. Should your school choose to abandon all these effective school-based measures and relieve Mr. Tai of his duties, as suggested by Mr. Leung in his open letter, not only will this severely undermine school autonomy, but it will also induce the undesirable chilling effect, imposing immense psychological burden on teachers and students, thus allowing white terror to descend upon everyone. As educators, giving in to the unreasonable requests of those in power is turning a blind eye to the injustice and inequality that threatens to tear our society apart. If you are to comply with such unjust commands, how are you to teach our youngsters the importance of virtues, of equality and of justice?
---------------------------仲有下一段，未完--------------------

----新一段---

Schools are where teachers demonstrate professionalism and where students learn without fear; teaching and learning go hand in hand. A teacher’s duty is not only to impart information and knowledge as written in textbooks - that is the most basic level. It is also to help students become open to reason, seek virtues and truth, tell right from wrong and learn from history. The anti-extradition-bill movement has given students an invaluable opportunity to understand, analyse and critique the voices of different stakeholders and communities in society. In particular, Mr Tai has set an example to students in learning from his mistakes and listening to public opinion humbly. May I ask which is more important and in line with educational goals: eliminating contrary opinions and smearing an educator who only slipped in the name of protection and social harmony, or teaching students how to live up to the ideals of truth, decency and justice?

In John Henry Newman’s The Idea of a University, he wrote ‘It is the education which gives a man a clear, conscious view of their own opinions and judgments, a truth in developing them, an eloquence in expressing them, and a force in urging them.’ Schools are a place that allows for mistakes and rectifications. This whole incident presents a perfect opportunity for students to learn from mistakes. Outside of schools, adults, who are supposed to be the role models for our future pillars of society, should lead by example: how to uphold moral standards, differentiate right from wrong, and do what is just. It is sincerely hoped that based on the Truth of the Bible and the benevolence of Jesus Christ, the founding principles of your school, you can make the right choice and actualize the essence of education.

Yours faithfully,
[Name]
[Title]

quote.msg:
香港新界將軍澳勤學里一號
香港華人基督教聯會真道書院
鍾嘉樂校監鈞鑒：

回應梁振英先生致信一事


　　久有仰聞　貴校自二零零二年建校至今，一直秉承創辦時的使命，不斷在學術與非學術方面追求進步，教學團隊專業，精益求精，配合現代教育趨勢的發展，追求卓越，桃李滿門，一直在區內享負盛名。

　　現今香港社會因修訂《逃犯條例》引起的風波未息，社會氣氛動盪，暴力加劇，社會撕裂。當權者未有聆聽市民訴求，一再回避是次政治議題之核心，以致事件演變成警民對立，衝突愈演愈烈，警民關係更接近仇視的狀態。作為一位專業的教育工作者，在大是大非之下更需抱著極為冷靜﹑理性及持平的角度分析現況，以發揮教師之專業。　貴校助理校長戴健暉先生發表意見的言辭過於偏激，有所失當是不爭的事實，雖其宣洩之言辭未有直接傷害他人，卻深化了雙方仇恨。惟戴老師後來收回言論並作公開致歉，已為此事負上了其作為教育工作者的社會責任，故及後校方如何處理事件應屬學校內務。

　　在將近兩個月的抗爭運動中，社會一直充斥着各種令人痛心疾首的事件：年輕人為爭取公義而受傷甚至犧牲性命，切盼可喚醒政府的良知; 無辜市民於公眾地方被懷疑三合會人士毆打，但警方視若無睹；警方使用不必要且不合比例的武力血腥鎮壓遊行人士的合理政治訴求，卻對眾目睽睽下手持武器的大批白衫人士並未採取即時的行動等等。一系列的事件為全港市民帶來一次又一次的痛心和震怒，即便如戴先生一名熱愛教育工作的人士，相信亦會感到悲哀及憤慨，情緒一時難以梳理亦能體諒。現階段實在不宜把事件的處理染上政治色彩，節上生枝，增添社會的負面情緒，作為教育工作者，我們更應關注莘莘學子潛藏的道德及情緒危機。

　　梁振英先生以香港特別行政區前行政長官及全國政協副主席落款，公然以職級身份施壓，態度囂張跋扈，更欲干涉學校內務，輕蔑校方的專業判斷。素仰 貴學一向本著管理自主，備有自我完善的工作循環，有策略地規劃工作及孕育反思文化，是促進學校持續發展和改善的關鍵，校內亦早已具備對教育工作者的評量標準，教師的專業及教學質素一直以來備受監督。如欲於此時因梁振英先生的一封公開信而革除 貴校助理校長戴健暉的職務，未免破壞了行之有效的校本管理，更會產生寒蟬效應，增添前線教育工作者及學子的心理壓力，製造白色恐怖。對梁卑躬屈膝不但令濫權者變本加厲，甚至破壞教育的本質，等同直接默許權貴間的迂腐及不公義，階級間的醜惡及不平等，試問日後將從何教授我們的下一代「社會人人平等，不分貧賤，互相體諒」的德行觀念？

　　學校於教育工作者而言是展現專業的地方，於學生而言是安心的學習環境，教學相長。教育工作者一直以授業解惑為己任，教學之道在乎明德理智，教授書本知識僅是基本，教授莘莘學子明辨事非，以史鑑今，抱誠守真，才是教育真正的追求。是次修例風波提供一個難得的平台供學子學習，嘗試了解、分析、體會及檢究各個不同持份者，社會上不同人士的聲音，而戴老師已為己誤作承擔，謙卑接受意見，已是最好的身教。難道排除異己，閉目塞耳，借故打擊一位失言的教育工作者，以保護之名隔絕及盲目地營造一個安穩的假象，比教授學生審時度勢，洞徹事理更為重要，更符合教育的本質？切望校方能慎思此事會為學生帶來怎樣的一課！

　　英國的若望‧亨利‧紐曼樞機（John Henry Newman）在《大學的觀念》一書中提及：「只有教育，才能使一個人對自己的觀點和判斷有清楚和自覺的認識；只有教育，才能令他/她闡明觀點時有道理，表達時有說服力，鼓動時有力量。」學校作為一個容許犯錯及改過的地方，是次事件正好教授學子面對問題的抗逆能力，學習何謂失敗者的優雅身姿；學校以外，成人的言行身教亦為重要，成人應以仁義之實踐為己任，以道德之展現為目標，才能言行均一讓學子建立合乎情理的個人價值觀，辨別是非，具「道之所在，雖千萬人吾往矣」的堅持。盼 貴校能本著以聖經真理辦學，以主耶穌的愛及寬恕待人處事，讓學生﹑教育工作者及香港市民藉以是次事件感受教育的美好及其真正的本質。

　 敬祝
均安
xxx敬上

二零一九年七月三十一日

二零一九年七月三十一日
語文科老師齊齊proofread下

July 31, 2019

Rev. Chung Ka Lok
School Supervisor
The Hong Kong Chinese Christian Churches Union Logos Academy
No. 1, Kan Hok Lane, Tseung Kwan O, Hong Kong

Dear Mr. Chung,
RE: CY Leung’s Letter

Since its establishment in 2002, HKCCCU Logos Academy has always adhered to its founding principles, striving for academic excellence and the provision of all-round education. Thanks to the unwavering dedication of professional teaching staff, your school is the cradle of social elite and is well reputed in Tseung Kwan O.

In the wake of the current social turmoil arising from the authority’s stubborn refusal to take into account society’s concerns about the extradition bill, violence has intensified and never has our society seen more animosity between citizens and the police. There is no denial that the opinions aired by your school's Assistant Principal Mr. Tai, which serve more as an outlet for anger than as a platform for rational discussion, are too radical to be within the confines of social acceptance. As a professional educator, Mr. Tai is expected to keep a cool head and analyse this issue in an objective manner, as opposed to venting his frustration. While his words may not have directly caused any harm, they do deepen hatred. Realising this, Mr. Tai has promptly retracted his speech and issued an apology, from which point the whole incident should be nothing more than the internal affairs of your school.

As the protests have now entered into the second month, more tragedies are also unfolding. More and more young adults demanding equal rights and justice have been injured during the protests. Some have even sacrificed their lives, in an attempt to rouse the government’s conscience. While innocent civilians were beaten by alleged triad members wearing white T-shirts in public areas, the police turned a blind eye and did not take any immediate actions. Instead, they used excessive force to disperse weaponless protesters. All this has saddened and infuriated Hongkongers. It is completely understandable that Mr Tai, a passionate educator, felt a deep sadness and bitter anger and reacted in a rather emotional manner. For the moment we should not make matters worse and pile more negative sentiments onto our society. We must take steps to defuse the moral and emotional crisis our students are suffering.

In Mr. Leung’s letter, which saw him openly addressing himself as Former Chief Executive of Hong Kong and vice chairperson of the Chinerse People’s Political Consultative Conference, he attempted to interfere with the school’s internal affairs by appealing to the prestigious status of his public office. One could not help but flinch at Mr. Leung’s arrogance. Schools should enjoy managerial autonomy so that it can implement its own framework of strategic planning and rigorous evaluation in order to facilitate sustainable development. And there are guidelines for teachers’ professionalism and teaching quality. Should your school choose to abandon all these effective school-based measures and relieve Mr. Tai of his duties, as suggested by Mr. Leung in his open letter, not only will this severely undermine school autonomy, but it will also induce the undesirable chilling effect, imposing immense psychological burden on teachers and students, thus allowing white terror to descend upon everyone. As educators, giving in to the unreasonable requests of those in power is turning a blind eye to the injustice and inequality that threatens to tear our society apart. If you are to comply with such unjust commands, how are you to teach our youngsters the importance of virtues, of equality and of justice?
---------------------------仲有下一段，未完--------------------


會唔會調一調轉次序好d 即第二第三段內容

----新一段---

Schools are where teachers demonstrate there professionalism and where students learn without any fear; teaching and learning go hand in hand. A teacher’s duty is not only to impart information and knowledge as written in textbooks - that is the most basic level. It is also to help students become open to reason, seek virtues and truth, tell right from wrong and learn from history. The anti-extradition-bill movement has given students an invaluable opportunity to understand, analyse and critique the voices of different stakeholders and communities in society. In particular, Mr Tai has set an example to students in learning from his mistakes and listening to public opinion humbly. May I ask which is more important and in line with educational goals: eliminating contrary opinions and smearing an educator who only slipped in the name of protection and social harmony, or teaching students how to live up to the ideals of truth, decency and justice?

In John Henry Newman’s The Idea of a University, he wrote ‘It is the education which gives a man a clear, conscious view of their own opinions and judgments, a truth in developing them, an eloquence in expressing them, and a force in urging them.’ Schools are a place that allows for mistakes and rectifications. This whole incident presents a perfect opportunity for students to learn from mistakes. Outside of schools, adults, who are supposed to be the role models for our future pillars of society, should lead by example: how to uphold moral standards, differentiate right from wrong, and do what is just. It is sincerely hoped that based on the Truth of the Bible and the benevolence of Jesus Christ, the founding principles of your school, you can make the right choice and actualize the essence of education.

Yours sincerely,
[Name]
[Title]
